hero.headline: Where the river bends, the wild begins
hero.imageAlt: Safari guests at sunset in the Maasai Mara, Exploreans Mara River Camp, Kenya

intro.label: The Camp
intro.headline: A front-row seat to one of Earth's greatest spectacles
intro.leadText: Perched on a graceful bend of the Mara River at the foot of the Oloololo Escarpment, the camp sits where comfort and the raw African wilderness dissolve into one another.
intro.bodyText: This is not a place designed to impress — it is a place that reveals itself. Elephants crossing the grounds at dusk. Hippos surfacing a stone's throw from your terrace. The escarpment catching the last light. Every moment here is unscripted, and that is precisely the point.
intro.imageAlt: Two guests observing a hippopotamus in the Mara River at Exploreans Mara River Camp during a guided wildlife experience

riverUsp.label: What makes us different
riverUsp.headline: You don't leave the camp to find the wild — it finds you
riverUsp.bodyText: Because the camp sits directly on the Mara River, wildlife is not something you drive to — it is something you wake up to. Elephants, giraffes, hippos, and crocodiles are daily visitors to the grounds.
riverUsp.imageAlt: Panoramic view of the Mara River and the surrounding wilderness at Exploreans Mara River Camp, where wildlife visits the grounds

stats.label: Tents & one cabin — intimate by design
Distance to wildlife — they come to you
From Kichwa Tembo Airstrip to camp

migration.label: When to Visit
migration.headline: Two million hooves. One river crossing.
migration.leadText: Every year between July and October, the Mara River becomes the stage for the greatest wildlife spectacle on the planet.
migration.bodyText: Vast herds of wildebeest and zebra gather at the banks, waiting for the moment to plunge into the current — braving crocodiles and the sheer force of the water. The camp's location near several major crossing points places you within reach of this drama.
migration.disclaimer: Peak migration season highlighted. River crossings are unpredictable and depend on herd movement and water levels.
migration.imageAlt: Wildebeest crossing the Mara River during the Great Migration, Maasai Mara Kenya

dining.label: Dining
dining.headline: Meals that belong to this place
dining.bodyText: Local, Asian, and international flavours — served in the garden, on the deck, or out in the bush. The restaurant changes with the day: continental breakfast at dawn, buffet under the trees at midday, à la carte under the stars.
dining.secondaryText: The Zebra Bar stays open until the last guest calls it a night — poolside by day, bonfire by evening.
dining.imageAlt: Outdoor bush dining under acacia trees at Exploreans Mara River Camp, Kenya

planVisit.label: Plan Your Visit
planVisit.headline: The Mara is waiting
planVisit.bodyText: Whether you're timing a visit around the Great Migration or looking for a quieter season, we'll help you find the right moment.

stay.label: Stay
stay.headline: Sleep where the river speaks
stay.subtitle: Three ways to stay — each one a different conversation with the Mara.
